growth_records.strain: BA1B borehole fluid community (250 m depth, Samail Ophiolite, Oman)
growth_records.medium: BA1B Type I Mg-HCO3- groundwater + 20 at.% D2O (unamended)

yields.biomass_yield_methanogens: 10^10-10^12 cells per mole CH4 (system-wide); max cell-specific CH4 production 517.9 ± 158.9 fmol CH4 cell-1 day-1, apparent Km(CO2aq)=2.3 µM (Monod fit across all BA conditions)

growth_records.cite: Kashyap et al. Microbial growth rates captured using Raman-SIP reveal a highly active subsurface biosphere fueled by serpentinization. Nature Communications (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41467-026-70622-w. [PMC13150008]
growth_records.snippet: we observed the highest median growth rates and shortest generation times (9 days) in the mildly alkaline fluid (BA1B)... BA1B-250 D2O median growth rate 0.0716 day-1, median generation time 9.0 d
growth_records.notes: Table 2. Median single-cell growth rate 0.0716 day-1 -> /24 = 0.002983 h-1. doubling_time = median generation time 9.0 d x 24 = 216 h. Mixed community, not pure culture; median reported directly (growth_method reported). Mean growth rate 0.0917 day-1, min 0.0012, max 0.619 day-1. Note median mu and median gen time are from separate distributions so mu*td != ln2.

growth_records.strain: BA1B borehole fluid community (250 m depth, Samail Ophiolite, Oman)
growth_records.medium: BA1B Type I Mg-HCO3- groundwater + 20 at.% D2O + 1 mM bicarbonate
growth_records.cite: Kashyap et al. Microbial growth rates captured using Raman-SIP reveal a highly active subsurface biosphere fueled by serpentinization. Nature Communications (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41467-026-70622-w. [PMC13150008]
growth_records.snippet: Cells exhibited even shorter generation times regardless of fluid type when amended with bicarbonate (6-123 days). BA1B-250 D2O+Bicarbonate median growth rate 0.0817 day-1, median generation time 5.8 d
growth_records.notes: Table 2. Median 0.0817 day-1 /24 = 0.003404 h-1. doubling = 5.8 d x 24 = 139.2 h. Mean 0.1212 day-1. Bicarbonate stimulated growth vs unamended (Tukey p<0.0001).

growth_records.strain: BA1B borehole fluid community (250 m depth, Samail Ophiolite, Oman)
growth_records.medium: BA1B Type I Mg-HCO3- groundwater + 20 at.% D2O + 1 mM formate
growth_records.cite: Kashyap et al. Microbial growth rates captured using Raman-SIP reveal a highly active subsurface biosphere fueled by serpentinization. Nature Communications (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41467-026-70622-w. [PMC13150008]
growth_records.snippet: Formate and acetate amendments stimulated growth rates of cells in BA1B. BA1B-250 D2O+Formate median growth rate 0.1129 day-1, median generation time 6.0 d
growth_records.notes: Table 2. Median 0.1129 day-1 /24 = 0.004704 h-1. doubling = 6.0 d x 24 = 144 h. Mean 0.1449 day-1, max 0.564.

growth_records.snippet: BA1B-250 D2O+Acetate median growth rate 0.1062 day-1, median generation time 6.3 d
growth_records.notes: Table 2. Median 0.1062 day-1 /24 = 0.004425 h-1. doubling = 6.3 d x 24 = 151.2 h. Mean 0.1252 day-1, max 0.998.

growth_records.strain: BA4A borehole fluid community (270 m depth, Samail Ophiolite, Oman)
growth_records.cite: Kashyap et al. Microbial growth rates captured using Raman-SIP reveal a highly active subsurface biosphere fueled by serpentinization. Nature Communications (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41467-026-70622-w. [PMC13150008]
growth_records.snippet: followed by moderately alkaline fluids (BA4A, 13 days). BA4A-270 D2O median growth rate 0.0430 day-1, median generation time 12.8 d
growth_records.notes: Table 2. Median 0.0430 day-1 /24 = 0.001792 h-1. doubling = 12.8 d x 24 = 307.2 h. Mean 0.0673 day-1, max 0.409. Carbon amendments had no significant effect in BA4A.

growth_records.strain: BA3A borehole fluid community (270 m depth, Samail Ophiolite, Oman; hyperalkaline Type II Ca-OH)
growth_records.medium: BA3A Type II Ca-OH groundwater + 20 at.% D2O (unamended)
growth_records.cite: Kashyap et al. Microbial growth rates captured using Raman-SIP reveal a highly active subsurface biosphere fueled by serpentinization. Nature Communications (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41467-026-70622-w. [PMC13150008]
growth_records.snippet: slowest growth rates and longest generation times (156 days) in the hyperalkaline fluids (BA3A). BA3A-270 D2O median growth rate 0.0008 day-1, median generation time 155.7 d
growth_records.notes: Table 2. Median 0.0008 day-1 /24 = 3.33e-5 h-1. doubling = 155.7 d x 24 = 3736.8 h. Mean 0.0076 day-1, max 0.250. Highest proportion of inactive cells in BA3A.

growth_records.strain: BA3A borehole fluid community (270 m depth, Samail Ophiolite, Oman; hyperalkaline Type II Ca-OH)
growth_records.medium: BA3A Type II Ca-OH groundwater + 20 at.% D2O + 1 mM bicarbonate
growth_records.cite: Kashyap et al. Microbial growth rates captured using Raman-SIP reveal a highly active subsurface biosphere fueled by serpentinization. Nature Communications (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41467-026-70622-w. [PMC13150008]
growth_records.snippet: Cells in BA3A only showed elevated growth in the bicarbonate amendment. BA3A-270 D2O+Bicarbonate median growth rate 0.0045 day-1, median generation time 123.0 d
growth_records.notes: Table 2. Median 0.0045 day-1 /24 = 1.875e-4 h-1. doubling = 123.0 d x 24 = 2952 h. Mean 0.0071 day-1, max 0.039. Bicarbonate significantly stimulated growth in hyperalkaline BA3A (Tukey p<0.0001).

growth_records.cite: Kashyap et al. Microbial growth rates captured using Raman-SIP reveal a highly active subsurface biosphere fueled by serpentinization. Nature Communications (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41467-026-70622-w. [PMC13150008]
growth_records.snippet: BA3A-270 D2O+Acetate median growth rate 0.0005 day-1, median generation time 573.2 d
growth_records.notes: Table 2. Median 0.0005 day-1 /24 = 2.08e-5 h-1. doubling = 573.2 d x 24 = 13756.8 h. Mean 0.0034 day-1, max 0.108. Slowest condition measured; acetate did not stimulate growth in BA3A.
